claimText: The Pollution Index (PI) for classifying industries ranges from 0 to 100 and is formulated based on the Precautionary Principle.
evidenceExcerpt: Pollution Index (PI) ranges from 0 to 100, formulated in 2016 based on the Precautionary Principle evaluating water, air, and hazardous waste.
examUse: Statement-based question on the methodology and guiding principle of the Pollution Index.
distractorHooks: Index ranges from 0 to 500 like AQI

claimText: Red Category industries have a Pollution Index greater than 80 and are normally not permitted in ecologically fragile or protected areas.
evidenceExcerpt: Red Category (PI > 80) industries are normally not permitted in ecologically fragile or protected areas.
examUse: Statement-based question on the restrictions applied to Red Category industries.

claimText: White Category industries have a Pollution Index below 25, are considered non-polluting, and do not require Environmental Clearance (EC) or Consent.
evidenceExcerpt: White Category (PI < 25) industries are non-polluting and do not require Environmental Clearance (EC) or Consent.
examUse: Statement-based or correct-pair question regarding exemptions for White Category industries.
distractorHooks: Require Consent to Establish but no EC
Green Category industries are exempt from EC

claimText: The Blue Category of industries includes Essential Environmental Services (ESSs) such as Municipal Solid Waste Management and sewage treatment plants.
evidenceExcerpt: Blue Category includes Essential Environmental Services (ESSs) like Municipal Solid Waste Management and sewage treatment plants.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching industry categories to their respective sectors.
distractorHooks: Blue Category is for water-intensive industries

claimText: The classification of industries based on pollution originated in 1989 with the Doon Valley Notification issued by the MoEFCC.
evidenceExcerpt: The classification of industries originated in 1989 with the Doon Valley Notification issued by MoEFCC.
examUse: Statement-based question on the historical origin of industry pollution classification in India.
distractorHooks: Originated with the establishment of the National Green Tribunal in 2010
